--- title: "Theme Test" subtitle: "Brand & Quarto Feature Showcase" author: - name: given: Mohamed family: Bakr url: https://portfolio.mohdbakr.com date: today date-modified: today abstract: | A comprehensive test page for verifying the site brand theme. Covers standard Quarto features: figures, tables, mathematics, callouts, code blocks, tabsets, mermaid diagrams, typography, layout, footnotes, and citations. keywords: - Quarto - Theme - Brand lang: en jupyter: python3 execute: warning: false message: false freeze: true copyright: "© 2026 Mohamed Bakr" references: - id: example_2024 author: - family: Example given: Jane - family: Author given: John container-title: Journal of Examples doi: 10.1234/example.2024 issued: 2024 title: "An Example Article for Demonstrating Citations" - id: canouil_2026 author: - family: Canouil given: Mickaël issued: 2026 title: "Branded Figures and Tables in R and Python with Quarto" url: https://mickael.canouil.fr/posts/2026-04-15-quarto-brand-figures-tables/ title-block-banner: true toc: true toc-title: Table of Contents --- ## Introduction {#sec-intro} This page tests every standard Quarto feature against the site brand theme. Use the table of contents to jump to a specific section. ### Document Organisation 1. **Quarto Features** (@sec-quarto): Figures, tables, mathematics, callouts, tabsets, and code blocks. 2. **Typography and Layout** (@sec-typography): Headings, lists, quotations, and multi-column layout. 3. **Advanced Features** (@sec-advanced): Footnotes, citations, and cross-references. ## Quarto Features {#sec-quarto} ### Figures {#sec-figures} #### Simple Figure ::: {#chart-example .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) apply_brand_theme(mode=mode) fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4)) ax.add_patch( plt.Rectangle( (0, 0), 1, 1, transform=ax.transAxes, facecolor=c["hero"], alpha=0.12, edgecolor="none", ) ) ax.text( 0.5, 0.55, "Figure Example", transform=ax.transAxes, ha="center", va="center", fontsize=22, fontweight="bold", color=c["hero"], ) ax.text( 0.5, 0.40, f"brand primary: {c['hero']}", transform=ax.transAxes, ha="center", va="center", fontsize=11, color=c["sub"], ) ax.set_xticks([]) ax.set_yticks([]) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-example-output-1.png){#chart-example-1 width=566 height=374} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-example-output-2.png){#chart-example-2 width=566 height=374} ::: ::: An example figure demonstrating basic figure capabilities with caption and cross-referencing. #### Subfigures ::: {#chart-multiple .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} panels = [ ("Panel A", "hero", "primary"), ("Panel B", "annot", "secondary"), ] for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) apply_brand_theme(mode=mode) fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(8, 3)) for ax, (label, key, tag) in zip(axes, panels): color = c[key] ax.add_patch( plt.Rectangle( (0, 0), 1, 1, transform=ax.transAxes, facecolor=color, alpha=0.15, edgecolor="none", ) ) ax.text( 0.5, 0.55, label, transform=ax.transAxes, ha="center", va="center", fontsize=18, fontweight="bold", color=color, ) ax.text( 0.5, 0.40, f"brand {tag}: {color}", transform=ax.transAxes, ha="center", va="center", fontsize=10, color=c["sub"], ) ax.set_xticks([]) ax.set_yticks([]) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-multiple-output-1.png){#chart-multiple-1 width=758 height=278} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-multiple-output-2.png){#chart-multiple-2 width=758 height=278} ::: ::: ### Tables {#sec-tables} #### Simple Table | Column A | Column B | Column C | |----------|----------|----------| | Value 1 | Value 2 | Value 3 | | Value 4 | Value 5 | Value 6 | | Value 7 | Value 8 | Value 9 | : A simple table demonstrating basic table capabilities. {#tbl-simple} #### Brand Colour Swatch {#sec-swatch} ::: {#chart-color-swatch .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) typo = _get_brand_typography(mode) apply_brand_theme(mode=mode) heading_font = typo.get("headings", typo.get("base", "sans-serif")) semantic = get_semantic_colors(mode) # deduplicate by hex value, keeping first occurrence seen = set() unique = [] for name, hex_val in semantic.items(): if hex_val not in seen: seen.add(hex_val) unique.append((name, hex_val)) # add foreground/background for name, hex_val in [("foreground", c["text"]), ("background", c["paper"])]: if hex_val not in seen: seen.add(hex_val) unique.append((name, hex_val)) n = len(unique) fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, max(2.5, n * 0.45))) for i, (name, hex_val) in enumerate(reversed(unique)): ax.barh(i, 1, color=hex_val, height=0.7, edgecolor="none") r, g, b = int(hex_val[1:3], 16), int(hex_val[3:5], 16), int(hex_val[5:7], 16) lum = 0.299 * r + 0.587 * g + 0.114 * b label_color = "#ffffff" if lum < 140 else "#1f2937" ax.text(0.5, i, f"{name} {hex_val}", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=10, color=label_color, fontweight="bold") ax.set_xlim(0, 1) ax.set_yticks([]) ax.set_xticks([]) ax.set_title( f"Brand Colour Swatch — {mode.title()} Mode", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", fontfamily=heading_font, color=c["accent"], loc="left", pad=10, ) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-color-swatch-output-1.png){#chart-color-swatch-1 width=950 height=250} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-color-swatch-output-2.png){#chart-color-swatch-2 width=950 height=250} ::: ::: ### Mathematics {#sec-maths} #### Inline Mathematics Inline expressions like $\alpha + \beta = \gamma$, $\int_0^1 f(x) \, dx$, and $\sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2}$ integrate seamlessly with text. #### Display Mathematics Einstein's mass-energy equivalence: $$ E = mc^2 $$ {#eq-einstein} The quadratic formula: $$ x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} $$ {#eq-quadratic} Bayes' theorem: $$ P(A \mid B) = \frac{P(B \mid A)\, P(A)}{P(B)} $$ {#eq-bayes} ### Callouts {#sec-callouts} ::: {.callout-note} Note callouts provide additional information or context without interrupting the main narrative flow. ::: ::: {.callout-tip} ## Pro Tip Tip callouts offer helpful suggestions, best practices, or recommendations. ::: ::: {.callout-important} ## Important Information Important callouts highlight essential details that readers must not miss. ::: ::: {.callout-warning} ## Warning Warning callouts alert readers to caveats, limitations, or potential issues. ::: ::: {.callout-caution} ## Caution Caution callouts warn about significant risks, dangers, or problematic scenarios. ::: ### Panel Tabsets {#sec-tabsets} ::: {.panel-tabset} #### Overview This tab contains introductory content. #### Python ```python # Bayes theorem example def bayes(p_a, p_b_given_a, p_b): return (p_b_given_a * p_a) / p_b posterior = bayes(0.01, 0.9, 0.11) print(f"P(A|B) = {posterior:.4f}") ``` #### R ```r # Bayes theorem in R bayes <- function(p_a, p_b_given_a, p_b) { (p_b_given_a * p_a) / p_b } posterior <- bayes(0.01, 0.9, 0.11) cat(sprintf("P(A|B) = %.4f\n", posterior)) ``` ::: ### Code Blocks {#sec-code} ```{.python filename="probability.py"} from scipy.stats import norm import numpy as np def confidence_interval(data, confidence=0.95): """Compute confidence interval for a sample mean.""" n = len(data) mean = np.mean(data) se = norm.ppf((1 + confidence) / 2) * (np.std(data, ddof=1) / np.sqrt(n)) return mean - se, mean + se ``` ```{.r filename="probability.R"} confidence_interval <- function(data, confidence = 0.95) { n <- length(data) mean_val <- mean(data) se <- qnorm((1 + confidence) / 2) * (sd(data) / sqrt(n)) c(mean_val - se, mean_val + se) } ``` ### Mermaid Diagrams {#sec-mermaid} ```{mermaid} %%| fig-cap: "A simple flowchart demonstrating Mermaid diagram support." %%| label: fig-mermaid flowchart LR A[Start] --> B{Decision} B -->|Yes| C[Action A] B -->|No| D[Action B] C --> E[End] D --> E ``` See @fig-mermaid for the rendered diagram. ### Code Annotations {#sec-annotations} ```python def calculate_area(radius: float) -> float: # <1> """Calculate the area of a circle.""" # <2> import math # <3> return math.pi * radius ** 2 # <4> ``` 1. Function signature with type hints for input and output. 2. Docstring describing the function purpose. 3. Import the `math` module for access to `pi`. 4. Apply the area formula: $A = \pi r^2$. ### Data Visualisation {#sec-viz} Four *Storytelling with Data* principles applied throughout: - **Title = insight**: headline states the finding, not just the topic - **Purposeful colour**: one hero hue; grey for everything else - **Minimal ink**: every gridline, spine, and tick must earn its place - **Direct labels**: text lives on the chart — no floating legend boxes Charts use transparent backgrounds and re-render per theme via `renderings: [light, dark]`. Colours are read at render time from `_brand.yml` via the `QUARTO_EXECUTE_INFO` environment variable (@canouil_2026). Derived tones (muted text, gridlines) are computed with `_col_mix()` so nothing is hardcoded — switching `_brand.yml` updates every output automatically. Tables use `great_tables` with a `gt_brand()` helper that applies brand colours and fonts. Because tables cannot use `renderings`, light and dark variants are wrapped in `.light-content` / `.dark-content` divs so the correct version shows for each theme. #### Learning Curve ::: {.panel-tabset} ##### Seaborn / Matplotlib ::: {#chart-learning-curve-mpl .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) typo = _get_brand_typography(mode) apply_brand_theme(mode=mode) hfont = typo.get("headings", typo.get("base", "sans-serif")) fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5)) ax.plot(weeks, weekly, color=c["muted"], linewidth=2.5, solid_capstyle="round", zorder=2) ax.plot(weeks, daily, color=c["hero"], linewidth=2.5, solid_capstyle="round", zorder=3) ax.fill_between(weeks, weekly, daily, alpha=0.07, color=c["hero"]) ax.text(8.2, 98, "Daily\npractice", color=c["hero"], va="center", fontsize=9, fontweight="bold", linespacing=1.4) ax.text(8.2, 71, "Weekly\nreview", color=c["muted"], va="center", fontsize=9, linespacing=1.4) ax.annotate( "Gap opens\nat week 4", xy=(4, 63), xytext=(1.8, 83), fontsize=9, color=c["secondary"], arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=c["secondary"], lw=1.2, connectionstyle="arc3,rad=-0.3"), ) for y in [25, 50, 75, 100]: ax.axhline(y, color=c["grid"], linewidth=0.7, zorder=0) ax.set_xlim(-0.3, 11.5) ax.set_ylim(0, 112) ax.set_xticks(weeks) ax.set_xticklabels(["Start"] + [f"Wk {w}" for w in weeks[1:]], fontsize=9) ax.set_yticks([0, 25, 50, 75, 100]) ax.set_yticklabels(["0 %", "25 %", "50 %", "75 %", "100 %"], fontsize=9) ax.tick_params(length=0) plt.tight_layout() plt.subplots_adjust(top=0.88, right=0.86) fig.text(0.01, 0.97, "Daily practice produces 38 % higher mastery", ha="left", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", fontfamily=hfont, color=c["text"]) fig.text(0.01, 0.92, "after eight weeks", ha="left", fontsize=11, fontfamily=hfont, color=c["sub"]) plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-learning-curve-mpl-output-1.png){#chart-learning-curve-mpl-1 width=754 height=485} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-learning-curve-mpl-output-2.png){#chart-learning-curve-mpl-2 width=754 height=485} ::: ::: ##### Plotly ::: {#chart-learning-curve-plotly .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) typo = _get_brand_typography(mode) hfont = typo.get("headings", typo.get("base", "sans-serif")) bfont = typo.get("base", "sans-serif") fig = go.Figure() fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=weeks, y=weekly, mode="lines", name="Weekly review", line=dict(color=c["muted"], width=2.5))) fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=weeks, y=daily, mode="lines", name="Daily practice", line=dict(color=c["hero"], width=2.5), fill="tonexty", fillcolor=f"rgba({int(c['hero'][1:3],16)},{int(c['hero'][3:5],16)},{int(c['hero'][5:7],16)},0.07)")) # Direct labels (match mpl) fig.add_annotation(x=8, y=98, text="Daily
practice", showarrow=False, xanchor="left", font=dict(color=c["hero"], size=10, family=bfont), xshift=8) fig.add_annotation(x=8, y=71, text="Weekly
review", showarrow=False, xanchor="left", font=dict(color=c["muted"], size=10, family=bfont), xshift=8) # Arrow annotation (match mpl) fig.add_annotation(x=4, y=63, ax=1.8, ay=83, text="Gap opens
at week 4", showarrow=True, arrowhead=2, arrowwidth=1.2, arrowcolor=c["secondary"], axref="x", ayref="y", font=dict(color=c["secondary"], size=10, family=bfont), xanchor="center", yanchor="bottom") fig.update_layout( title=dict( text=("Daily practice produces 38 % higher mastery
" "after eight weeks"), font=dict(family=hfont, size=14, color=c["text"]), x=0.01, xanchor="left", ), font=dict(family=bfont, color=c["text"]), xaxis=dict(title=dict(text="Week", font=dict(family=hfont)), gridcolor=c["grid"], zeroline=False, color=c["sub"], range=[-0.3, 11.5]), yaxis=dict(title=dict(text="Mastery %", font=dict(family=hfont)), gridcolor=c["grid"], zeroline=False, color=c["sub"]), plot_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)", paper_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)", showlegend=False, margin=dict(t=70, b=40, l=60, r=20), height=450, hovermode="x unified", hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor=c["paper"], font_color=c["text"], bordercolor=c["grid"]), ) fig.show() ``` ::: {#chart-learning-curve-plotly-1 .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: ::: #### Common Misconceptions ::: {.panel-tabset} ##### Seaborn / Matplotlib ::: {#chart-misconceptions-mpl .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) typo = _get_brand_typography(mode) apply_brand_theme(mode=mode) hfont = typo.get("headings", typo.get("base", "sans-serif")) colors = [c["hero"]] + [c["muted"]] * (len(pct) - 1) fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5)) bars = ax.barh(range(len(concepts)), pct, color=colors, height=0.55) for bar, val, col in zip(bars, pct, colors): weight = "bold" if val == max(pct) else "normal" ax.text( bar.get_width() + 1.5, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2, f"{val} %", va="center", fontsize=10, color=col, fontweight=weight, ) ax.set_yticks(range(len(concepts))) ax.set_yticklabels(concepts, fontsize=10) ax.invert_yaxis() ax.xaxis.set_visible(False) ax.tick_params(left=False, bottom=False) ax.set_xlim(0, 110) plt.tight_layout() plt.subplots_adjust(top=0.88) fig.text(0.01, 0.97, "Gambler's Fallacy is the most persistent misconception", ha="left", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", fontfamily=hfont, color=c["text"]) fig.text(0.01, 0.92, "probability misconceptions survey", ha="left", fontsize=11, fontfamily=hfont, color=c["sub"]) plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-misconceptions-mpl-output-1.png){#chart-misconceptions-mpl-1 width=860 height=484} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-misconceptions-mpl-output-2.png){#chart-misconceptions-mpl-2 width=860 height=484} ::: ::: ##### Plotly ::: {#chart-misconceptions-plotly .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=11} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) typo = _get_brand_typography(mode) hfont = typo.get("headings", typo.get("base", "sans-serif")) bfont = typo.get("base", "sans-serif") bar_colors = [c["hero"]] + [c["muted"]] * (len(pct) - 1) fig = go.Figure(go.Bar( y=concepts, x=pct, orientation="h", marker_color=bar_colors, text=[f"{v} %" for v in pct], textposition="outside", textfont=dict(color=bar_colors), )) fig.update_layout( title=dict( text=("Gambler's Fallacy is the most persistent misconception
" "probability misconceptions survey"), font=dict(family=hfont, size=14, color=c["text"]), x=0.01, xanchor="left", ), font=dict(family=bfont, color=c["text"]), xaxis=dict(visible=False), yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed", title="", tickfont=dict(family=bfont), color=c["sub"]), plot_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)", paper_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)", showlegend=False, margin=dict(t=70, b=20, l=200, r=40), height=450, ) fig.show() ``` ::: {#chart-misconceptions-plotly-1 .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: ::: #### Bayesian Updating ::: {.panel-tabset} ##### Seaborn / Matplotlib ::: {#chart-bayesian-mpl .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=12} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) typo = _get_brand_typography(mode) apply_brand_theme(mode=mode) hfont = typo.get("headings", typo.get("base", "sans-serif")) fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5)) traces = [ (2, 8, c["muted"], "Prior Beta(2, 8)"), (7, 13, c["secondary"], "10 obs Beta(7, 13)"), (22, 28, c["hero"], "40 obs Beta(22, 28)"), ] for a, b, color, label in traces: ax.plot(x_beta, beta_dist.pdf(x_beta, a, b), color=color, linewidth=2.5, label=label) ax.axvline(0.44, color=c["sub"], linewidth=1, linestyle=":") ax.text(0.46, ax.get_ylim()[1] * 0.9, "true rate 0.44", fontsize=8, color=c["sub"]) ax.set_xlabel("Estimated success rate", fontfamily=hfont) ax.set_ylabel("Probability density", fontfamily=hfont) ax.legend(frameon=False, fontsize=9, loc="upper left") ax.set_axisbelow(True) ax.grid(True, axis="x", color=c["grid"], linewidth=0.5) plt.tight_layout() plt.subplots_adjust(top=0.88) fig.text(0.01, 0.97, "More evidence pulls beliefs toward the true rate", ha="left", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", fontfamily=hfont, color=c["text"]) fig.text(0.01, 0.92, "Bayesian updating with Beta-Binomial model", ha="left", fontsize=11, fontfamily=hfont, color=c["sub"]) plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-bayesian-mpl-output-1.png){#chart-bayesian-mpl-1 width=860 height=483} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-bayesian-mpl-output-2.png){#chart-bayesian-mpl-2 width=860 height=483} ::: ::: ##### Plotly ::: {#chart-bayesian-plotly .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=13} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} def _trace(a, b, color, name): return go.Scatter(x=x_beta, y=beta_dist.pdf(x_beta, a, b), mode="lines", name=name, line=dict(color=color, width=2.5)) for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) typo = _get_brand_typography(mode) hfont = typo.get("headings", typo.get("base", "sans-serif")) bfont = typo.get("base", "sans-serif") fig = go.Figure([ _trace(2, 8, c["muted"], "Prior Beta(2, 8)"), _trace(7, 13, c["secondary"], "10 obs Beta(7, 13)"), _trace(22, 28, c["hero"], "40 obs Beta(22, 28)"), ]) fig.add_vline(x=0.44, line_dash="dot", line_color=c["sub"], annotation_text="true rate 0.44", annotation_position="top right", annotation_font_color=c["sub"]) fig.update_layout( title=dict( text=("More evidence pulls beliefs toward the true rate
" "Bayesian updating with Beta-Binomial model"), font=dict(family=hfont, size=14, color=c["text"]), x=0.01, xanchor="left", ), font=dict(family=bfont, color=c["text"]), xaxis=dict(title=dict(text="Estimated success rate", font=dict(family=hfont)), tickformat=".0%", showgrid=True, gridcolor=c["grid"], zeroline=False, color=c["sub"]), yaxis=dict(title=dict(text="Probability density", font=dict(family=hfont)), showgrid=False, zeroline=False, color=c["sub"]), legend=dict(x=0.02, y=0.98, bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)", font=dict(color=c["text"])), plot_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)", paper_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)", margin=dict(t=70, b=40, l=60, r=20), height=450, hovermode="x unified", hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor=c["paper"], font_color=c["text"], bordercolor=c["grid"]), ) fig.show() ``` ::: {#chart-bayesian-plotly-1 .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: ::: *Drag the Plotly chart to explore how a sceptical prior (Beta(2,8)) narrows toward the true success rate as observations accumulate.* #### Probability Distributions — Branded Table {#sec-branded-table} Tables styled with `gt_brand()` inherit the active brand's colours, fonts, and palette automatically. Because `great_tables` does not support the `renderings` cell option, light and dark variants are wrapped in `.light-content` / `.dark-content` divs (@canouil_2026). ::: {.light-content} ::: {#tbl-distributions-light .cell tbl-cap='Common probability distributions and their key properties (light mode).' execution_count=15} ``` {.python .cell-code} gt_brand(dist_data, brand_mode="light") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=13} ```{=html}
Distribution Notation Mean Variance Support
Normal N(μ, σ²) μ σ² (−∞, ∞)
Beta Beta(α, β) α / (α + β) αβ / ((α+β)²(α+β+1)) [0, 1]
Exponential Exp(λ) 1 / λ 1 / λ² [0, ∞)
Poisson Pois(λ) λ λ {0, 1, 2, …}
Binomial Bin(n, p) np np(1 − p) {0, 1, …, n}
``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.dark-content} ::: {#tbl-distributions-dark .cell tbl-cap='Common probability distributions and their key properties (dark mode).' execution_count=16} ``` {.python .cell-code} gt_brand(dist_data, brand_mode="dark") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=14} ```{=html}
Distribution Notation Mean Variance Support
Normal N(μ, σ²) μ σ² (−∞, ∞)
Beta Beta(α, β) α / (α + β) αβ / ((α+β)²(α+β+1)) [0, 1]
Exponential Exp(λ) 1 / λ 1 / λ² [0, ∞)
Poisson Pois(λ) λ λ {0, 1, 2, …}
Binomial Bin(n, p) np np(1 − p) {0, 1, …, n}
``` ::: ::: ::: #### Sample-Size vs. Power ::: {.panel-tabset} ##### Seaborn / Matplotlib ::: {#chart-power-curve-mpl .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=17} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) typo = _get_brand_typography(mode) apply_brand_theme(mode=mode) hfont = typo.get("headings", typo.get("base", "sans-serif")) pal = c["palette"] fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5)) for idx, (label, d) in enumerate(effect_sizes.items()): se = 1 / np.sqrt(n_sample) z_crit = sp_norm.ppf(0.975) power = 1 - sp_norm.cdf(z_crit - d / se) color = pal[idx % len(pal)] ax.plot(n_sample, power * 100, color=color, linewidth=2.5, solid_capstyle="round") ax.fill_between(n_sample, 0, power * 100, alpha=0.05, color=color) ax.text(203, power[-1] * 100, label, color=color, va="center", fontsize=9, fontweight="bold" if idx == 0 else "normal") ax.axhline(80, color=c["grid"], linewidth=1.2, linestyle="--", zorder=0) ax.text(12, 82, "80 % convention", fontsize=8, color=c["sub"]) for y in [25, 50, 75, 100]: ax.axhline(y, color=c["grid"], linewidth=0.5, zorder=0) ax.set_xlim(10, 230) ax.set_ylim(0, 105) ax.set_xticks([10, 50, 100, 150, 200]) ax.set_yticks([0, 25, 50, 75, 100]) ax.set_yticklabels(["0 %", "25 %", "50 %", "75 %", "100 %"], fontsize=9) ax.set_xlabel("Sample size (n)", fontsize=10, fontfamily=hfont) ax.tick_params(length=0) plt.tight_layout() plt.subplots_adjust(top=0.88, right=0.80) fig.text(0.01, 0.97, "Larger effects reach 80 % power with far fewer observations", ha="left", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", fontfamily=hfont, color=c["text"]) fig.text(0.01, 0.92, "statistical power by effect size", ha="left", fontsize=11, fontfamily=hfont, color=c["sub"]) plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-power-curve-mpl-output-1.png){#chart-power-curve-mpl-1 width=727 height=483} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/chart-power-curve-mpl-output-2.png){#chart-power-curve-mpl-2 width=727 height=483} ::: ::: ##### Plotly ::: {#chart-power-curve-plotly .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=18} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) typo = _get_brand_typography(mode) hfont = typo.get("headings", typo.get("base", "sans-serif")) bfont = typo.get("base", "sans-serif") pal = c["palette"] fig = go.Figure() for idx, (label, d) in enumerate(effect_sizes.items()): se = 1 / np.sqrt(n_sample) z_crit = sp_norm.ppf(0.975) power = 1 - sp_norm.cdf(z_crit - d / se) color = pal[idx % len(pal)] fig.add_trace(go.Scatter( x=n_sample, y=power * 100, mode="lines", name=label, line=dict(color=color, width=2.5), )) # Direct label at right edge (match mpl) fig.add_annotation(x=200, y=float(power[-1] * 100), text=label, showarrow=False, xanchor="left", xshift=5, font=dict(color=color, size=10, family=bfont)) fig.add_hline(y=80, line_dash="dash", line_color=c["grid"], annotation_text="80 % convention", annotation_font_color=c["sub"]) fig.update_layout( title=dict( text=("Larger effects reach 80 % power with far fewer observations
" "statistical power by effect size"), font=dict(family=hfont, size=14, color=c["text"]), x=0.01, xanchor="left", ), font=dict(family=bfont, color=c["text"]), xaxis=dict(title=dict(text="Sample size (n)", font=dict(family=hfont)), gridcolor=c["grid"], zeroline=False, color=c["sub"], range=[10, 260]), yaxis=dict(title=dict(text="Power %", font=dict(family=hfont)), gridcolor=c["grid"], zeroline=False, color=c["sub"]), plot_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)", paper_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)", showlegend=False, margin=dict(t=70, b=40, l=60, r=20), height=450, hovermode="x unified", hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor=c["paper"], font_color=c["text"], bordercolor=c["grid"]), ) fig.show() ``` ::: {#chart-power-curve-plotly-1 .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: ::: #### Penguins Scatter ::: {.panel-tabset} ##### Seaborn / Matplotlib ::: {#chart-penguins-mpl .cell renderings='["light","dark"]' execution_count=19} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} markers_penguin = ["o", "^", "s"] for mode in ["light", "dark"]: c = get_chart_colors(brand_mode=mode) typo = _get_brand_typography(mode) apply_brand_theme(mode=mode) hfont = typo.get("headings", typo.get("base", "sans-serif")) pal = c["palette"] fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5)) for i, (species, grp) in enumerate(penguins.groupby("species")): ax.scatter( grp["bill_length_mm"], grp["bill_depth_mm"], c=pal[i % len(pal)], marker=markers_penguin[i % len(markers_penguin)], label=species, s=30, alpha=0.85, ) ax.set_xlabel("Bill length (mm)", fontfamily=hfont) ax.set_ylabel("Bill depth (mm)", fontfamily=hfont) ax.legend( title="Species", loc="upper left", title_fontproperties={"family": hfont, "weight": "bold"}, ) ax.set_axisbelow(True) ax.grid(True, color=c["grid"], linewidth=0.5) plt.tight_layout() plt.subplots_adjust(top=0.88) fig.text(0.01, 0.97, "Bill dimensions separate penguin species cleanly", ha="left", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", fontfamily=hfont, color=c["text"]) fig.text(0.01, 0.92, "Palmer Penguins dataset", ha="left", fontsize=11, fontfamily=hfont, color=c["sub"]) plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output go.Figure() for i, species in enumerate(penguins["species"].unique()): grp = penguins[penguins["species"] == species] fig.add_trace(go.Scatter( x=grp["bill_length_mm"], y=grp["bill_depth_mm"], ``` ::: ::: ::: ## Typography and Layout {#sec-typography} ### Heading Levels {#sec-headings} This section demonstrates heading hierarchy. ### Level 3 Heading Content under level 3 heading demonstrating hierarchy and spacing. #### Level 4 Heading Content under level 4 heading with appropriate font sizing. ##### Level 5 Heading Content under level 5 heading maintaining visual consistency. ### Lists {#sec-lists} #### Unordered Lists - First item demonstrating unordered list styling. - Second item with nested elements: - Nested item 2.1 showing second-level indentation. - Nested item 2.2 with consistent formatting. - Third item returning to top level. #### Ordered Lists 1. First numbered item establishing sequence. 2. Second numbered item with sub-steps: a. Sub-step 2.1 using alphabetic numbering. b. Sub-step 2.2 maintaining hierarchy. 3. Third numbered item completing the sequence. ### Quotations {#sec-quotes} > "The goal is to turn data into information, and information into insight." > > — Carly Fiorina ### Layout Features {#sec-layout} #### Multi-Column Layout ::: {layout-ncol=2} **Column 1:** Content in the first column demonstrating side-by-side layout capabilities. **Column 2:** Content in the second column showing parallel information. ::: ## Advanced Features {#sec-advanced} ### Footnotes {#sec-footnotes} This sentence includes a footnote.[^1] Another sentence demonstrates multiple footnotes working together.[^2] [^1]: First footnote providing additional context or explanatory information. [^2]: Second footnote demonstrating how multiple footnotes coexist. ### Citations and Bibliography {#sec-citations} Citations integrate seamlessly with the bibliography system. See @example_2024 for a comprehensive example of citation formatting. ### Cross-References {#sec-refs} **Sections:** @sec-intro, @sec-quarto, @sec-figures, @sec-tables, @sec-maths. **Figures:** @fig-mermaid. **Tables:** @tbl-simple, @tbl-distributions-light, @tbl-metrics-light. **Equations:** @eq-einstein, @eq-quadratic, @eq-bayes. ## Conclusion {#sec-conclusion} This page exercises all standard Quarto features for visual verification of the brand theme. Toggle between light and dark mode to confirm both variants render correctly. ### Brand Colours Applied See the dynamic **Brand Colour Swatch** (@sec-swatch) in the Tables section above — it renders the active `_brand.yml` semantic colours at build time. All charts and branded tables read these values automatically; switching to a different brand file updates every output. ### Render ```bash quarto render example.qmd ``` ## References {.references} ::: {#refs} :::
